item.title: Uveitis in spondyloarthritis: clinical patterns and differences between axial and peripheral forms.
item.description: Clinical rheumatology - The association between uveitis and spondyloarthritis is well established. However, its characterization across the different types of spondyloarthritis remains limited in the literature. Therefore...

item.title: Pyoderma Gangrenosum: Association of Ulcer Size With Clinical Characteristics and Healing Outcomes.
item.description: The Australasian journal of dermatology - Pyoderma gangrenosum (PG) is an ulcerative inflammatory dermatosis that is poorly characterised from a diagnostic and therapeutic perspective. We evaluated the clinical characteristics and healing ...

item.title: The at-risk-to-early continuum of enteropathic spondyloarthritis.
item.description: The Lancet. Rheumatology - Review - Enteropathic spondyloarthritis is a common, under-recognised musculoskeletal manifestation of inflammatory bowel disease and a potentially preventable contributor to long-term disability. Epidemiol...

item.title: No serological association between Porphyromonas gingivalis or Prevotella intermedia and early spondyloarthritis: analysis from the DESIR cohort.
item.description: Clinical and experimental medicine - Porphyromonas gingivalis and Prevotella intermedia are key oral pathogens implicated in periodontal disease and have been associated with rheumatoid arthritis and specific categories of juvenile id...

item.title: Clinical Decision-Making and Multidisciplinary Management of Peristomal Pyoderma Gangrenosum in Stage IVB Rectal Cancer: A Case Report-Corticosteroid Response but Fatal Cancer Progression.
item.description: Reports (MDPI) - Case report - Background and Clinical Significance: Peristomal pyoderma gangrenosum (PPG) is a rare subtype of pyoderma gangrenosum, most commonly associated with inflammatory bowel disease or haematologic disor...

item.title: Aberrant p53 overexpression in benign colon biopsies may predict dysplasia risk in patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis and inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Histopathology - Patients with primary sclerosing cholangitis (PSC) and inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), collectively termed PSC-IBD, have an increased risk of developing nonconventional and/or invisible colorecta...

item.title: HIF-1α in macrophage polarization: roles in immunometabolism and autoimmune diseases.
item.description: Journal of leukocyte biology - Hypoxia-inducible factor-1α (HIF-1α) is a central regulator of cellular responses to hypoxia and plays a pivotal role in immune cell activation and functional reprogramming. This review summarizes ...

item.title: [A potpourri of diagnoses-Unusual manifestations of a chronic inflammatory bowel disease : Case report].
item.description: Zeitschrift fur Rheumatologie - Inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) are frequently associated with extraintestinal manifestations, which usually occur concomitantly with or following onset of an intestinal disease. The presence of ...

item.title: Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome and X-linked thrombocytopenia: a review of the clinical and immunological spectrum with a case presentation highlighting glomerulonephritis.
item.description: Frontiers in pediatrics - Review - Wiskott-Aldrich syndrome (WAS) is a rare X-linked primary immunodeficiency caused by mutations in the WAS gene, characterized by thrombocytopenia, eczema, recurrent infections, and immune dysregula...
